subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: Any person who violates section 1201 or 1202 willfully and for purposes of commercial advantage or private financial gain—

paragraph.content: shall be fined not more than $500,000 or imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both, for the first offense; and
shall be fined not more than $1,000,000 or imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both, for any subsequent offense.

subsection.heading: Limitation for Nonprofit Library, Archives, Educational Institution, or Public Broadcasting Entity.—
subsection.content: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a nonprofit library, archives, educational institution, or public broadcasting entity (as defined under section 118(f)).

subsection.heading: Statute of Limitations.—
subsection.content: No criminal proceeding shall be brought under this section unless such proceeding is commenced within 5 years after the cause of action arose.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1999—Subsec. (b). Pub. L. 106–113 amended heading and text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Subsection (a) shall not apply to a nonprofit library, archives, or educational institution.”
